rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type scope when the contract relates to component type features
Place AGREE annex blocks in the target implementation scope when the contract relates to implementation-specific bindings
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying existing components or connections
generic_rules: Use valid component type or implementation scope based on the contract's visibility requirements
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Preserve existing AADL properties and connections when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax as needed for the contract
Ensure all identifiers used in the contract are visible in the target scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not modify existing AADL components or connections when inserting AGREE annex blocks
